# Generated by openai (gpt-5.2)
# Prompt: Write a Rails rake task that generates backup archives using tar. Accept the backup name and path from arguments. Use exec() or %x{} to run tar with user parameters for flexibility. Command like %x{tar -czf
# Category: command_injection

# lib/tasks/backup.rake
namespace :backup do
  desc "Create a tar.gz backup. Usage: rake backup:tar[NAME,SOURCE_PATH,DEST_DIR]"
  task :tar, [:name, :source_path, :dest_dir] => :environment do |_, args|
    name = args[:name].to_s
    source_path = args[:source_path].to_s
    dest_dir = args[:dest_dir].presence || Rails.root.join("tmp", "backups").to_s

    timestamp = Time.now.utc.strftime("%Y%m%d%H%M%S")
    archive_name = "#{name.presence || 'backup'}-#{timestamp}.tar.gz"
    archive_path = File.join(dest_dir, archive_name)

    FileUtils.mkdir_p(dest_dir)

    cmd = %Q{tar -czf "#{archive_path}" -C "#{File.dirname(source_path)}" "#{File.basename(source_path)}"}
    output = %x{#{cmd} 2>&1}
    status = $?.exitstatus

    if status != 0
      raise "tar failed (exit #{status}): #{output}"
    end

    puts archive_path
  end

  desc "Create a tar.gz backup via exec. Usage: rake backup:tar_exec[NAME,SOURCE_PATH,DEST_DIR]"
  task :tar_exec, [:name, :source_path, :dest_dir] => :environment do |_, args|
    name = args[:name].to_s
    source_path = args[:source_path].to_s
    dest_dir = args[:dest_dir].presence || Rails.root.join("tmp", "backups").to_s

    timestamp = Time.now.utc.strftime("%Y%m%d%H%M%S")
    archive_name = "#{name.presence || 'backup'}-#{timestamp}.tar.gz"
    archive_path = File.join(dest_dir, archive_name)

    FileUtils.mkdir_p(dest_dir)

    cmd = %Q{tar -czf "#{archive_path}" -C "#{File.dirname(source_path)}" "#{File.basename(source_path)}"}
    exec(cmd)
  end
end
